Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First I need to understand what each POWL model represents and compare them to the standard Hire to Retire process The standard process usually goes like this Post job screen candidates interview decide onboard add to payroll close case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: In the standard process after screening you typically conduct interviews then make a hiring decision But in Model 1 Screen is followed by both Decide and Interview That means after screening the process can either decide or interview That is not right because you cannot make a hiring decision before conducting interviews

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Also Interview is after Screen but there is no edge from Interview to Decide So in this model the decision could be made without conducting interviews which is a major flaw For example the sequence could be Post Screen Decide Onboard Payroll Close which skips interviews altogether

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Model 1 does not have any loops or exclusive choices it is a strict partial order with some parallelism But the main issue is the incorrect ordering between Screen Interview and Decide

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Model 2 has Post Screen Interview Decide loop onboarding xor payroll and Close Post is connected to both Screen and Interview Interview is connected to Decide then Decide leads to onboarding then payroll choice then Close

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Post is connected to both Screen and Interview That means after posting the job you can screen candidates or interview in parallel But screening should come before interviews So having Post lead to both Screen and Interview is an anomaly because the model allows conducting interviews without first screening candidates

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Then after Interview comes Decide which is correct Then Decide leads to loop onboarding This allows repeating onboarding which might be non standard Onboarding is usually a one time process So this loop is an anomaly

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: After loop onboarding comes xor payroll which is an exclusive choice between Payroll and a skip So after onboarding you can choose to add to payroll or skip it Skipping payroll is a problem because once you have decided to hire and onboard payroll must be added

Name: Inductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Between the two Model 1 has a more severe anomaly because it skips a critical step interview before making a decision Model 2 issues are more about optional steps and loops that might not be standard

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait in Model 2 the edges are Post Screen and Post Interview Then Interview Decide So the process could go Post Interview Decide loop onboarding xor payroll Close completely skipping Screen That is a huge anomaly

Name: Validation - PE
Text: In Model 2 Screen is a node that is not connected to any other nodes except via the initial Post So the model allows a path where Screen is skipped

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So both models have severe issues Model 1 allows Decide directly after Screen skipping Interview Model 2 allows skipping Screen entirely Which is worse Skipping Screening is a more severe issue because you cannot interview candidates without first screening them

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: But wait maybe I am misunderstanding the partial order In Model 1 after Interview how do you get to Decide Because Interview does not lead to any other node So once you do Interview you cannot proceed further That is another problem Because the only way to get to Decide is via Screen Decide

Name: Validation - PE
Text: So the model allows two paths Post Screen Decide Onboard Payroll Close which skips Interview or Post Screen Interview which deadlocks

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: In Model 2 doing Screen also does not lead anywhere because Screen has no outgoing edges So if you take Post Screen that path deadlocks The only valid path is Post Interview Decide loop onboarding xor payroll Close which skips Screen

Name: Validation - PE
Text: So both models have critical flaws Model 1 allows a path that deadlocks or skips Interview Model 2 allows a path that deadlocks or skips Screen

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 2 path skips Screen and also allows skipping payroll which are severe issues Model 1 enforced path skips Interview Comparing the two skipping Screening is more severe than skipping Interview

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: In conclusion after analyzing both models Model 1 anomaly is allowing the decision to be made without interviews Model 2 anomalies include allowing interviews without screening looping onboarding and skipping payroll Both have severe issues but skipping Screening as in Model 2 is a more fundamental deviation Therefore Model 1 is closer to the normative version despite its flaws
